data.body:
REBAR-DEBUG DIAGNOSIS (2026-07-21)

Reproduction/localization: src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py always invokes llm.verify_completion(ticket_id, graph=False, source="attested", ref="HEAD") for a gated non-force close after structural prechecks. src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md requires every literal acceptance criterion plus the bug-resolution question to be met. It explicitly says a codebase-verifiable criterion is never satisfied by a ticket comment alone.

Root cause: the completion model has only MET/NOT MET over the original criterion text. The lifecycle has no audited "moot/invalidated/superseded criterion" state and no automation-owned alert closure branch. Therefore evidence that disproves the ticket premise cannot reclassify a codebase criterion; it can only fail it, leaving force-close as the sole route.

Minimal corrective seam: introduce an explicit, auditable criterion-disposition/alert-ownership contract before verification, bind it to concrete evidence and authorized provenance, and teach the verifier and close gate to distinguish completed, invalidated/moot, and automation-owned criteria without accepting free-form PASS commands. Regression coverage should include the f436 false-positive alert and reject unsupported moot claims.

No code changed during this diagnostic run.
